title: An End-to-End Detection and Segmentation Model for Container Surface Damage Based on YOLOv11
authors: Wenjie Su, Ningyao Yu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: To address the problems of low efficiency, strong subjectivity, and poor robustness of traditional manual inspection methods for container surface damage, this study proposes an end-to-end detection and segmentation model based on YOLOv11 for container surface damage. The model aims to accurately locate damage regions and identify damage types (dent, hole, rusty) in complex port environments. By integrating an improved CSP-ResNeXt backbone, BiFPN feature fusion module, ECA attention mechanism, and decoupled detection head, the model enhances the ability to extract multi-scale features and resist environmental interference. Experimental results on a dataset containing 3713 container images show that the model achieves mAP@ 0.5 of 0.750, mAP@0.5:0.95 of 0.450, average Precision of 0.780, and Recall of 0.720 on the validation set. Among the three damage types, dent achieves the highest mAP of 0.820, and the Dice coefficient for rusty segmentation reaches 0.89. Comprehensive performance evaluation verifies the model's excellent precision, real-time performance, and generalization ability, providing a reliable technical solution for automated container damage inspection in ports.

title: Case Studies and Real-World Applications of AI-Generated Content in User Interface Design in Smartphones
authors: Ting Zhang, Si Xie
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Artificial intelligence has been integrated into the field of art design, and the processes and methods of UI design have also changed. Studying the application of artificial intelligence-generated content technology in UI design can save time and manpower, allowing designers to relieve themselves from tedious work. Detach and focus on creative design. After integrating AI technology into the project development requirements, details, design, development, testing, operation stage, iteration, and other stages, by showing the design process of a study check-in APP, I learned about the methods used by UI designers and AI in the collaboration process. Platforms, tools, and techniques, and discusses the changing role of the UI designer. UI designers still need to be responsible for requirements analysis, research analysis, test feedback, etc., and they need to effectively cooperate and communicate with AI in prototype generation, personalized design, auxiliary design, evaluation design, etc. However, the current level of AI does not replace the role of UI designers, but becomes a partner or assistant to designers, helping them complete design tasks faster and better.

title: A Hybrid Informer-LightGBM Framework for Enhanced Carbon Emission Forecasting in Industrial Parks
authors: Xingyun Yan, Jie Hu, Lingyu Wang, Mingzhu Fang, Hairui Wang, Meixing Guo, Bin Chen, Jin Qi
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Accurate short-term forecasting of net purchased power is essential for estimating carbon emissions in electricity-intensive industrial parks. However, existing methods often struggle to capture the nonlinear and dynamic nature of electricity consumption, resulting in persistent residual errors. This paper presents a novel two-stage hybrid framework that integrates an optimized Informer model with LightGBM-based residual learning. The Informer captures long-range temporal dependencies, while LightGBM corrects systematic residuals using a carefully engineered feature set. Validation using six years of data from a Shanghai industry-academia-research innovation park demonstrates superior performance: the Informer-LightGBM hybrid achieves a MAPE of 5.94%, reflecting a 2.3% relative improvement over the standalone Informer and substantial gains over traditional approaches.

title: An Efficient FPGA-Based AES Encryption Design for IoT Terminals: Low-Power Optimization with a BRAM-Based S-Box
authors: Xinyan Liu, Youwen Cui, Yunhua He
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: To meet the dual demands of encryption security and low power consumption in Internet of Things (IoT) terminals during data transmission, this paper proposes a Field-Programmable Gate Array (FPGA)-based Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) encryption scheme that employs block random access memory (BRAM) to implement the S-box. Conventional FPGA-based AES designs typically use look-up tables (LUTs) to implement the S-box, leading to excessive dynamic power consumption from high-frequency logic switching and inefficient resource utilization. To overcome these issues, the proposed design innovatively stores 256 groups of 8-bit input-output substitution values of the S-box in BRAM in advance. Owing to the static nature of BRAM, logic toggling is significantly reduced. In addition, low-power techniques such as clock gating and stable address signals are integrated. Furthermore, a custom polynomial are applied to generate the S-box substitution table, thereby enhancing performance. Experimental results confirm that the proposed scheme achieves an optimal balance for IoT terminals, reducing dynamic power consumption by 59.6 % compared to the conventional design while maintaining robust security performance.

title: Depression Detection in Social Media Comments Based on BiGRU-CapsNet
authors: Hongfu Tian
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: With the development of social media, user online comments have gradually become an important data source for depression tendency detection. However, existing models fail to fully explore contextual information and contextual dependencies in social media comments, face issues with ambiguity in emotional words, and often lose important information during the extraction process. To address these issues, a depression tendency detection model for social media comments based on BiGRU-CapsNet is proposed. The model utilizes a dual BiGRU network to capture contextual information and contextual dependencies from both forward and backward directions, and inputs them into semantic capsule networks and sentiment capsule networks to extract high-level feature representations, enhancing the model's ability to capture key information. An attention mechanism is then introduced to perform weighted fusion of the dual feature representations, followed by classification using a softmax classifier. Experimental results on the public datasets eRisk2017 and eRisk2018 show that the proposed model significantly outperforms mainstream models such as BiLSTM, BiGRU, and BERT in terms of F1 score and AUC, achieving an F1 score of 0.823 and an AUC of 0.941. Further ablation experiments validate the critical role of the capsule network structure, BiGRU modeling, and the semantic and sentiment dual branches in improving model performance, demonstrating the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed approach in depression tendency detection tasks.

title: Local Flexibility Markets: Coordination of Distributed Flexibility Resources: European and U.S. Experiences and Innovations in Distributed Energy Resources
authors: Hossein Farahmand, Alex Farley, Masood Parvania
abstract: Distributed Energy Resources (DERS) have emerged as a fundamental component in modern energy systems, promoting decentralization, sustainability, and resilience. Establishing flexibility markets has become crucial in the global transition to a low-carbon future to optimize the integration and coordinate the utilization of DERs. This article provides a comparative analysis of local flexibility market developments in Europe and the United States, offering insights into innovative technologies, policy frameworks, business models, and practical experiences.

title: The Effect of Different Learning Rates on the Performance of Face Recognition
authors: Yingbo Zang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: Facial Expression Recognition (FER) represents a significant computer vision challenge, with diverse applications spanning healthcare, security, and human-computer interaction. This investigation explores the influence of the learning rate-a crucial but frequently undervalued hyperparameter - on convolutional neural network (CNN) efficacy in FER tasks. A specialized dual-pathway CNN architecture was implemented on the FER-2013 dataset, designed to simultaneously process raw pixel information and enhanced edge features. Experimental trials compared learning rates set at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.1,0.01$</tex>, and 0.001. Findings revealed that a learning rate of 0.01 produced optimal outcomes, attaining a weighted <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 6 2}$</tex> and an accuracy rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 6 3}$</tex>, accompanied by stable training dynamics and enhanced detection of high-arousal emotional states like “happy” and “surprise”. Conversely, a rate of 0.1 induced significant training instability and convergence failure, while 0.001 resulted in prolonged convergence and diminished performance. This research emphasizes the critical role of learning rate calibration in FER systems and establishes that intermediate values promote effective model training without overfitting or oscillation. The study provides actionable insights for hyperparameter optimization in emotion detection networks, delivering practical guidelines for developing efficient deep learning solutions. Additionally, it identifies imperative directions for addressing dataset imbalance and integrating adaptive learning mechanisms in next-generation FER frameworks.

title: Ethical and Security Challenges of Large Language Models in Public Health Systems: A Survey
authors: Jie He, Fengshi Jing, Deyi Liang, Hao Ren, Qimeng Luo, Yewei Xie, Haobin Zhang, Weibin Cheng
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: The integration of large language models (LLMs) into public health systems promises significant improvements in disease surveillance, clinical communication, and health education. However, their deployment raises fundamental ethical and security risks-ranging from data privacy breaches to algorithmic bias and misinformation. This survey systematically reviews recent literature to elucidate these challenges, categorize emerging risks, and assess current evaluation and governance mechanisms. Our findings highlight the urgent need for interdisciplinary frameworks that embed ethical principles throughout the LLM lifecycle to ensure trustworthy and equitable AI integration in health care.

title: Mixed-Signal Modulation Recognition Based on Feature Fusion Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Shiran Zhang, Yawei Ji, Mingyang Yang, Junning Tang, Yizhe Zhang, Zongfu Xie, Qingxin Huang, Haoran Zhao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: In the modern wireless communication environment, multi-user co-frequency transmission and electromagnetic interference lead to the prevalence of mixed signals, which poses severe challenges of feature overlap and noise interference for automatic modulation recognition. To this end, this paper proposes a mixed-signal modulation recognition network (FFCNN) that integrates a feature fusion neural network with complexvalued convolution and spectral feature extraction. This model takes I/Q complex signals and high-order spectra as inputs, extracts frequency features through complex-valued convolutional layers and convolutional layers respectively, and enhances feature expression through feature fusion. The simulation results show that the neural network architecture proposed in this paper can effectively improve the performance of mixed-signal modulation recognition.

title: Glacier Melt Time Series Prediction Model Based on LSTM Neural Network
authors: Shan Fangyue, Shao Yiran
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: In light of this context, there arises the need to apply an efficient model for time series prediction from the melting process of the glaciers in order to better understand the temperature-driven acceleration trend of the melting process. Based on the principles of LSTM networks, this paper recommends the proposal of an LSTM model for predicting the melting process of the glaciers in order to quantify the temperature-driven acceleration trend of the melting process, where the proposed model would yield root mean square error, mean absolute error, and coefficient of determination of values 0.178 m w.e., 0.142 m w.e., and 0.92, respectively, with 57% and 38% improvement in ARIMA model and standard RNN model, respectively.

title: Application of Natural Language Processing in the Analysis of Emotional Logs in Music Therapy
authors: Zichen Wang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: This paper investigated the use of natural language processing (NLP) methods for mood diary analysis in the context of music therapy. We presented a model combining BERT and LSTM for an efficient emotion classification process. The proposed model correctly identified the emotion semantics expressed in the mood diaries using the attention mechanism and the gate architecture, implemented through preprocessing, word segmentations, and feature extraction. The experiment outcome also verified that the combination model performed better in accuracy, precision, recall, and F 1 measure than the separate models, ensuring that it is reliable and accurate for emotion recognition. The proposed study also evaluated the use of music type in emotion regulation, offering valuable insights for digital intervention in the mental health domain.

title: Application of Deep Reinforcement Learning in FPGA Multi-Frequency Clock Phase-Shifting Adaptive Measurement
authors: Zhao Yulong, Wang Jianxiang, Wang Qian, Wang Dongjun, Cai Zhiwei, Wang Kuan
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: This paper, in view of traditional FPGA clock frequency phase shift in a dynamic environment adaptive measuring method, precision limited problem, this study proposes a reinforcement learning based on depth (DRL) adaptive measurement algorithm. By constructing a decision-making framework for the interaction between the agent and the environment, a multi-dimensional state space is designed, which includes clock frequency characteristics, phase deviation indicators, environmental interference parameters and system resource states, and a discrete-continuous hybrid action space is determined, such as phase adjustment and measurement strategy selection. As the core, combined with the phase deviation both reward function, resource consumption and environmental interference ResNet and attention mechanism of hybrid neural network is adopted to improve the end-to-end optimization. The experimental results show that compared with traditional method, the DRL will measure precision up to 30 ps, precision is 47%. At the same time, through the model pruning and knowledge distillation technology, the computing resource consumption is reduced by 63%. The algorithm in dynamic change frequency clock jitter and + 15 ps environment conditions show the strong robustness, precision measuring equipment for high performance computing and provides a high precision, low utilization of adaptive measuring solution.

title: Extending Cyber Threat Modeling with Physical Dependency Analysis in DC Microgrids
authors: Saqib Ali, Andrii Chub, Hayretdin Bahsi, Shaymaa Mamdouh Khalil
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: The cybersecurity of Direct Current Microgrids (DC-MGs) is becoming increasingly critical due to their integration of tightly coupled physical and cyber components. This convergence introduces new vulnerabilities that expose the system to both cyber and physical threats, necessitating comprehensive and robust security measures. Appropriate Threat Modeling (TM) is needed to identify, analyze, and address potential threats. Traditionally, TM approaches only consider cyber threats and neglects composite physical dependencies between cyber systems and physical processes. This study extends cyber threat modeling with a more comprehensive impact assessment that considers the physical dependencies for cyber-physical system security analysis of DC-MGs, which many traditional models overlook. For this purpose, we proposed DC-MG architecture as a case study, drew a data flow diagram, mapped trust boundaries, implemented a threat modeling approach, and accounted for the impact assessment approach by considering physical dependencies. This yields a comprehensive analysis that identifies cyber threats, their physical dependencies, impact, and consequences for resilient DC-MGs to enable the development of reliable energy infrastructures.

title: Secure Storage Strategies for Massive Data in Network Computing Environments
authors: Yan Xu, Hongmei Liu, Xinrui Gong, Zeren Fan
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: In recent years, the advancement of computer technology has significantly enhanced data computing capabilities in network environments. The advent of the big data era has transformed information in daily life, work, and study into digital correlation chains. Network-based resource sharing simplifies complex learning tasks and improves work/study efficiency. However, traditional network storage strategies frequently encounter issues such as data overflow leaks and storage server security vulnerabilities. To address these challenges, this study proposes a secure large-capacity data storage strategy for network computing environments, which dynamically incorporates Data Correction Units (DCU), Logic Compensation Units (LCU), and Node Encryption Units (NEU) to effectively resolve limitations of conventional approaches. Simulation experiments demonstrate that this strategy features fast response times, strong penetration resistance, high logical consistency, robust data security, and user-friendly operation.

title: GNN-ETF: A Graph Neural Network-Based Framework for Modeling Inter-Sector Relationships in ETFs
authors: Hailong Li, Yangyang Xie, Xueliang Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: The integration of multiple data entities can support objectives like pattern analysis and structured representation. Since individual entities often represent aggregated collections, they inherently address specific variations. Extending this, further exploration can involve constructing models of exchangetraded funds (ETFs), leading to the concept of ETF relational modeling. Traditional optimization methods, such as performance metrics, often assume independence among assets. However, sectors are typically interconnected, and identifying as well as quantifying these links is key for ETF model construction. Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) offer a flexible approach for modeling and measuring relationships among nodes. By treating ETFs from different sectors as nodes, and their co-movement patterns at the same time point as weighted edges, we combine these associations with a performance metric to develop a GNNETF model, aiming to identify an optimized ETF configuration.

title: Autonomous Systems - Digitalization, Education and Innovation in the engineering education
authors: Jelena Caiko, Anastasija Zhiravecka, Nadezhda Kunicina, Vladimir Beliaev
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: With the increasing deployment of autonomous systems in infrastructures, ensuring optimal control of electrotechnical systems is a top priority. This paper explores control challenges in autonomous systems and presents an innovative educational approach aimed at digitalization of course content quipping future engineers with the necessary skills. The Education project brings together three universities to create a teaching ecosystem focused on digital competences in main fields. Energy efficiency, and intelligent system design, cybercritical systems, drones and other applications are analyzed as case studies. By integrating hands-on workshops, industry collaborations, and digitalization, the initiative enhances students’ practical expertise and prepares them for addressing emerging security threats in autonomous technologies. Furthermore, the project fosters research and knowledge exchange on advanced control methods, including AI-driven threat detection, secure IoT implementation, and regulatory compliance.

title: Analysis of User-Privacy, Third-Party Data Sharing and Consent Mechanism on Online Pharmacy Websites in Ontario
authors: Atikor Ibodeng, Zuhaibuddin Bhutto, Yan Yan, Sampsa Rauti, Ville Leppänen, Adegboola David Adelabu, Wenjun Lin
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: The purpose of this study is to evaluate the privacy practices of online pharmacy websites in Ontario, Canada, focusing on data sharing with third parties, privacy policies, and the effectiveness of consent mechanisms. To achieve this, we conducted a comprehensive analysis using both automatic and manual network traffic analysis methods. We analyzed the traffic flow of 109 pharmacy websites, assessing the presence of third-party services, the transmission of data to third parties, and the availability and clarity of privacy policies and consent forms. Our study indicates that a significant number of pharmacy websites in Ontario share sensitive health information with third-party entities, such as Google Analytics and Facebook, even without explicit user consent. Additionally, many of these websites lack comprehensive privacy policies and effective consent mechanisms. We aim to create awareness of the limitations of third-party data sharing, the adoption of clearer and more accessible privacy policies, and robust consent mechanisms crucial for protecting the privacy of user data.

title: CTD-YOLO: An Efficient Object Detection Algorithm for Tiny Surface Defects on Cranes
authors: Xinren Wang, Wensheng Su, Yanzu Wei, Dongming Hu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: To address the dual challenges of capturing tiny, low-contrast surface defects (such as weld cracks and rust) and the limited computational resources of edge devices in crane safety inspection, this paper proposes CTD-YOLO, a lightweight and efficient object detection algorithm based on the YOLOv8 architecture. First, to enhance the feature extraction of weak defects against complex steel backgrounds, we construct a lightweight backbone incorporating GSConv and a novel C2f_SCB module, which fuses Star Operation with dual attention mechanisms to achieve high-dimensional non-linear mapping in low-dimensional space. Second, to ensure real-time inference on edge devices, the neck network is reconstructed using C2f_RepGhost modules based on structural re-parameterization, effectively eliminating multi-branch computational redundancy via a training-inference decoupling strategy. Finally, a P2 detection head specialized for tiny targets is introduced alongside a lightweight detection head structure (Detect_LW) to utilize shallow high-resolution features, fundamentally resolving the high missed detection rate of fine cracks. Experimental results on a specialized crane defect dataset demonstrate that CTD-YOLO achieves a mean Average Precision (mAP @ 0.5) of 84.5 %, significantly outperforming the baseline YOLOv8n, particularly in weld crack detection (+10.7 %). Furthermore, the model maintains a parameter size of only 2.9 M and an inference speed of 135 FPS, achieving an optimal balance between high precision and efficiency suitable for deployment on UAVs and climbing robots.

title: Adjustable-Error-Based Adaptive Neural Network Tracking Control for Uncertain Nonlinear Systems
authors: Faxiang Zhang, Yu Shi, Jing Na, Pak Kin Wong, Guanbin Gao, Jing Zhao, Yingbo Huang, Pengshuai Dai
abstract: This article proposes an adjustable-error neural network (NN) approximator and incorporates it into the adaptive neural tracking controller design of uncertain nonlinear systems. Noted that the error between the unknown nonlinear function and the NN approximator cannot be adjusted under the traditional NN control framework, as it is solely determined by the selection of neurons, basis functions, and the estimation of the ideal weight vector. This inherent constraint compromises the precision of the NN approximation and the convergence accuracy of the tracking error. To improve the approximation accuracy of unknown nonlinear functions in adaptive neural control systems, an adjustable-error NN approximator is designed, in which the error between the approximator and the unknown nonlinear function can be adjusted by designed parameters. Based on the proposed NN approximator, an adaptive neural tracking controller is designed for a class of uncertain nonlinear systems, which achieves higher accuracy of the tracking error compared with traditional methods. The stability of the resulting closed-loop system is proved in the Lyapunov sense, and the convergence of the tracking error is also analyzed. The effectiveness of the proposed scheme is verified by simulation and experiment.

title: Interference Free Multi-User Coded ISAC with Physical Layer Security
authors: Sumeyra Hassan, Negin Kazemipourleilabadi, Ibrahim Kahraman, Yalcin Sadi, Mutlu Koca, Erdal Panayirci, H. Vincent Poor
publish: IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Communications
abstract: This paper proposes a multi-user integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) framework based on coded generalized spatial shift keying (MU-CGSSK) that enables secure, interference-free downlink transmission alongside high-resolution radar sensing. The system adopts a separated deployment model, allowing simultaneous communication and sensing over a shared frequency band using co-located antenna arrays. A systematic linear block code is employed to generate structured antenna activation patterns that ensure low bit error rates and optimal MU separation. A key feature is the proposed two-stage physical layer security (PLS) scheme. In the first stage, a channel state information (CSI)-based precoder eliminates MU and radar-induced interference while generating implicit jamming against eavesdroppers. The second stage enhances confidentiality under partial CSI leakage by applying lightweight scrambling to antenna index mappings. An alternating optimization algorithm jointly designs the precoding vector and power allocation to maximize the achievable secrecy sum-rate. Simulation results demonstrate that the MU-CGSSK ISAC system achieves reliable MU communication, accurate multi-target detection using the MUSIC algorithm, and robust secrecy performance under asymmetric channel conditions.

title: Tx Coaxial Stepped Impedance Resonator Filters With Distinct Transmission Zeros
authors: Alejandro Buitrago-Bernal, Jessica Benedicto, Jean-François Favennec, Langis Roy, Eric Rius
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: Quarter-wavelength three-section coaxial stepped impedance resonators (SIRs), having two degrees of freedom, allow independent control of the first resonance and antiresonance. Utilizing an original interconnection and based on polarity inversion, it is possible with this kind of resonator to design the nth-order bandpass filters offering n distinct transmission zeros in the upper stopband. To validate the principle, we designed and fabricated a demonstrator three-section fourth-order filter in aluminum at L-band, for which we also simulated the multipactor threshold. The experimental results are presented and discussed.

title: A Proton Irradiation Damage Compact Model of HfO x /TiO x Heterojunction Memristive Synapses for Hardening Design of Convolutional Neural Network Hardware
authors: Shengju Yin, Hongjia Song, Chuan Ma, Jiaqi Yan, Chenfeng Lin, Jinbin Wang, Xiangli Zhong
abstract: Memristive neural network hardware shows great potential for Space applications thanks to its high efficiency, low power, high integration, and radiation tolerance. Yet no memristor model captures the influence of irradiation effects on its conductance update behavior, impeding the radiation-hardening design of memristive neural network hardware. This article presents a proton irradiation damage compact model of memristive synapses. Simulations of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$I$</tex-math> </inline-formula>–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$V$</tex-math> </inline-formula> curves and conductance-update linearity agree with experiment within an rms error <0.2. Using this model, we design a radiation-hardened synaptic circuit that reduces long-term potentiation/depression nonlinearity by 42% pre- and 33% postirradiation. A convolutional neural network (CNN) built with these radiation-hardened synapses retains 95%/92% recognition accuracy pre-/postirradiation, surpassing single-memristor networks (93%/88%). The model and circuit form a foundation for Space-grade memristive neural network hardware.

title:
The Electromagnetic Properties of Multi-filaments MgB
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Wires
authors: Qiang Guo, Mingjiang Wang, Wenlong Yuan, Wei Xie, Yanmin Zhu, Bo Wu, Yigong Shi, Jianfeng Li, Xianghong Liu, Yong Feng, Pingxiang Zhang
abstract: Reducing the AC loss of MgB2 wire is significant for its possible AC engineering applications. In this study, the multi filaments MgB2 wires possessed of various filaments diameter were fabricated via in-situ PIT (Powder in Tube) method. The effects of filaments diameters on the superconducting properties and magnetic hysteresis loss of MgB2 wires were studied systematically. With the filaments diameters decrease from 120 μm to 40 μm, the critical current density (Jc) decreases by 20% (4.2 K, 3 T), the critical transition temperature (Tc) shows a consistent trend, while the magnetic hysteresis losses (Qm) decrease from 1230 mJ/cm3 to 650 mJ/cm3. The above results demonstrate that the smaller filaments diameter is beneficial to reduce the Qm of MgB2 wire, but the attenuation of current capacity can also not be ignored

title: Spectral Koopman-Hopf Formula
authors: Sarang Sutavani, Sriram S. K. S. Narayanan, Umesh Vaidya
abstract: We propose a novel spectral Koopman-Hopf (SKH) formula to solve finite-time optimal control problems with input constraints for control-affine nonlinear systems. This method leverages the spectral properties of the Koopman operator to transform state-dependent Hamiltonians, providing an exact viscosity solution under strict assumptions or provable value function bounds in the general case, thereby extending the applicability of the standard Hopf formula. This approach transforms the curse of dimensionality into a curse of complexity, utilizing a path-integral formula to compute eigenfunctions, which mirrors the grid-free, parallelizable nature of the Hopf formula itself. The framework demonstrates superior global convergence on a 2D system and a 3× speedup on a 4D robotic arm, compared to nonlinear MPC by reducing the problem to a low-dimensional convex optimization.

title: Powering Reliability, Engineering Resilience: A Unified Vision for the Modern Grid [Editor’s Voice]
authors: Innocent Kamwa

title: Biodegradable Nanorobots Meet Edge Computing: Sustainable Intelligence in the Soil
authors: Arya Upadhyay
abstract: This article explores the integration of biodegradable nanorobots and edge computing as a sustainable approach to environmental monitoring. Traditional cloud-based systems consume extensive energy for data transmission and computation, creating a paradox where digital progress contributes to ecological degradation. By embedding computation directly into eco-friendly, biodegradable robotic systems, engineers can achieve localized, energy-efficient intelligence that minimizes environmental impact. This article presents the principles, architecture, applications, and ethical considerations of biodegradable nanorobots powered by edge intelligence, emphasizing their potential in soil restoration, pollution detection, and sustainable agriculture.

title: Investigation of Load Parameters of a Hybrid Stepper Motor
authors: Vytenis Sinkevicius, Valda Sneideriene
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: In studies of the dynamic behavior of hybrid stepper motors, it is essential to know the load parameters. This paper presents a method for calculating the moment of inertia, dry friction torque, and viscous friction coefficient from the free oscillations of the motor or its load shaft. The example uses the mechanism of a two-wheeled mobile robot sensor platform driven by a hybrid stepper motor.

title: Real-Time Grasping Detection Algorithm Based on Improved YOLO11
authors: Changjiang Jiang, Fenglei Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: To address the challenge of achieving efficient grasping by robotic arms in unstructured environments, a real-time grasp detection algorithm is proposed.The algorithm uses YOLO11 as its basic framework, introducing LDConv into the backbone network to achieve network lightweighting and enhance the model's adaptability to detection targets of varying sizes;The CA module is integrated into the feature fusion stage to suppress redundant features and reinforce the selection of key information;Finally, the SIoU loss function replaces CIoU, optimizing the regression accuracy of rotated bounding boxes through the introduction of an angle loss.This method achieves an Image-wise segmentation accuracy of 98.2 %, an Object-wise segmentation accuracy of 97.6 %, and a grasp detection speed of 63.1 FPS on the Cornell dataset, demonstrating clear advantages compared to other classic methods.

title: An Improved Typhoon Track Prediction Method Based on Conditional Diffusion Model
authors: Jingchang Li, Xiuqing Hu, Shaolin Wang, Feng Sang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Accurate typhoon track forecasting is critical for disaster prevention and mitigation. Numerical weather prediction (NWP) models are computationally expensive and sensitive to initial-condition errors, while many deep-learning approaches still often fail to fully exploit spatiotemporal dependencies when fusing multimodal predictors. We propose TYPCD, a conditional diffusion—based framework for probabilistic typhoon track prediction. TYPCD encodes historical tracks and ERA5 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$500-\text{hPa}$</tex> geopotential height fields into a unified conditional representation via a multimodal conditional encoder. A diffusion transformer with Adaptive Layer Normalization (AdaLN-DiT) injects the conditional vector into each denoising block, enabling dynamic and stage-wise constraint throughout the generation process. Experiments on the CMA BST + ERA5 dataset show that TYPCD reduces the Mean Distance Error (MDE) by 19.71%, 23.86%, 16.47%, and 12.06 % at 6, 12, 18, and 24 h lead times compared with TC-Diffuser, while remaining lightweight enough to run on a single consumer GPU with 1. 5 s inference per case.

title: Performance Evaluation of Plant Leaf Disease Detection using Structural Modification of the Traditional Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Apoorva Sharma, Kalpna Guleria, Shagun Sharma
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: Plant diseases reduce agricultural productivity, and farmers struggle to precisely identify and control these diseases which leads to crop losses. The early identification of plant diseases is a challenging task in agriculture due to the variation in the size, shape, colour, and texture caused by environmental changes. This research employs convolutional neural networks (CNNs) for the identification of plant leaf diseases, namely, corn common rust, potato early blight, and tomato bacterial spot. The model was trained with the augmented dataset on 15 epochs with and without the additional convolution layer in the CNN architecture. The accuracy achieved by CNN without adding additional layers is 99.4% and after adding additional layers, the accuracy is 75.5% at 10 epoch. These results show that the proposed method can be optimized in the future and can be used as the most effective method in predicting plant leaf disease. This model may lead to enhanced crop yields and quality by rapid prediction of leaf diseases.

title: A Cross-Language Information Retrieval and Knowledge Graph Construction Method for Chinaafrica Economic and Trade Cooperation
authors: Jianjun Huang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: This study systematically expounds the methods of cross-language information retrieval and knowledge graph construction for China-Africa economic and trade cooperation. By breaking through language barriers through key technologies such as semantic vector representation, query optimization strategies, and automatic translation, and combining graph-structured neural networks and high-dimensional vector similarity calculations, a knowledge network construction framework supporting multi-language interaction is designed. It has significantly enhanced the cross-language integration efficiency of the knowledge network, providing an effective solution for information query and knowledge organization in China-Africa economic and trade cooperation, and demonstrating broad application prospects.

title: The Impact of Gamification and Blended Learning Platform on Engagement: The Mediating Role of Course Satisfaction
authors: Zhiying Tan, Thanawan Phongsatha, Meiling Liang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: With the rapid evolution of blended learning, one persistent challenge is the decline of Chinese students' engagement over time. However, there is a lack of comprehensive models addressing the influencing factors within vocational education. This study addresses this gap by integrating students' perceptions of blended learning platforms and gamification as critical determinants of learning engagement. Employing a mixed-method design, quantitative data were collected from 774 freshmen in a vocational college, and the qualitative insights were gathered through offline semi-structured interviews with 16 students. Structural equation modeling was applied to examine the hypothesized relationships. The findings revealed that both gamification and perceptions of the blended learning platform significantly influenced students' engagement, with course satisfaction serving as a mediating factor. Furthermore, the study highlights avenues for future research on integrating gamification and platform perception into pedagogical models to sustain student engagement.

title: Application Process of Intelligent Target Detection Algorithm in Complex Background for Skywave Over-the-Horizon Radar
authors: Dawei Wang, Wei Jiang, Zhiyuan Shi, Yu Fang, Kun Lu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: Skywave over-the-horizon radar can detect extremely long-range targets by utilizing the ionospheric reflection mechanism. However, in complex clutter and interference environments, traditional target detection methods based on constant false alarm rate (CFAR) are limited in performance due to their reliance on accurate estimation of noise and clutter distributions. To improve detection accuracy, reduce clutter interference in target recognition, and thereby enhance real-time performance, this paper proposes a staged model training process suitable for skywave radar, validated using the YOLOv8 object detection model, achieving end-to-end target recognition on radar Range-Doppler (RD) maps. Firstly, the characteristics of typical clutter such as sea clutter, ionospheric disturbances, and radio frequency interference, as well as their manifestations in RD maps, are analyzed. Based on this, an annotated dataset for skywave radar scenarios is constructed, and annotation principles and methods adapted to complex interference environments are proposed. Training and validation are conducted according to the established process. Experimental results indicate that this method can achieve robust target detection under different clutter backgrounds, possesses high versatility in the field of radar, and significantly improves detection efficiency and automation levels.

title: LLM-driven Task Dispatching Heuristic Design for Multi-Agile Earth Observing Satellites Scheduling
authors: Feiran Wang, Xiaolu Liu, Yuning Chen, Yingwu Chen
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: To address the time-consuming and complex multiple agile Earth observation satellite scheduling problem (multi-AEOSSP), we propose a novel application of the LLMassisted evolutionary computation (LLM-EC) framework to design task dispatching heuristics with three key innovations: 1) Customized prompt strategies for initialization, crossover, and mutation tailored for efficient order-dispatching heuristics. 2) The evolved heuristics decompose multi-AEOSSP into parallel single-satellite subproblems, significantly reducing solution complexity. 3) Experiments conducted on realistic scenarios demonstrate that LLM-EC achieves a higher solution quality than expert-designed heuristics, with improvements of up to 6.8%.

title: Optimization of the Semantic Matching Module of the Automatic English Composition Correction System
authors: Lijie Su
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: With the development of educational technology, the automatic correction system for English compositions has gradually been applied, especially showing obvious advantages in marking efficiency and personalized feedback. However, the current scoring techniques still do not perform well enough in terms of language relevance issues, such as semantic sentence recognition and context understanding. This study proposes a method based on deep learning and dynamic word embedding to enhance the accuracy of the semantic matching module. By enhancing context awareness and dynamic representation of vocabulary, the upgraded system can better identify polysemous words, synonymous sentences and complex sentences in compositions, thereby improving the accuracy of scores. After experimental testing, this optimization method can achieve better results in both semantic matching accuracy and stability, which is conducive to providing more accurate automated scoring feedback.

title: Application of Supercapacitors to Extend the Critical Clearing Time by Time Domain Equal Area Criterion
authors: Navid Bayati, Thomas Ebel
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: In recent years, the integration of renewable energy resources has increased in power systems. Typically, renewable energy resources are connected to the main power system through power electronic devices. It could reduce the inertia of power systems, and consequently, jeopardize the stability of power systems. In this paper, the application of supercapacitors in power systems to provide virtual inertia and enhance system stability is investigated through numerical analysis of the equal area criterion in the time domain. As a result, the critical clearing time improvement of power systems by using supercapacitors is numerically calculated. The effectiveness of the proposed solution is validated through PowerFactory and OPAL-RT through laboratory tests. Results show that the implementation of supercapacitors could enhance the critical clearing time at least by 300 ms in the IEEE 39-bus New England test system.

title: Brain Tumor Detection Using Advanced Deep Learning Models - Vision Transformer and EfficientNetV2
authors: Yang Liu, Li-Jun Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Advanced deep learning model - EfficientNetV2 is reported that the model has achieved a better performance than the Vision Transformer (ViT) for some simple tests in the original research recently. However, it is still now clear whether the EfficientNetV2 performs better than the ViT for practical applications. We will study the two advanced deep learning models for brain tumor image detection. In addition, we will use one robust machine learning model – eXtreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) as benchmark to study how much the two deep learning models perform better than the benchmark. The EfficientNetV2 performed best (98.57% accuracy) among the three models for the brain tumor image detection and the model was very stable based on p-value statistics (p-value <0.05) in our tests.

title: MGACMR: Multi-Granularity Feature Adversarial Cross-Modal Retrieval Model
authors: Xiaorong Zhang, Sipeng Deng, Jiakang Deng, Cheng Chen, Guijuan Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Existing cross-modal retrieval methods based on subspace networks lack mutual guidance and collaborative learning between different modalities when applied to the imagetext retrieval of multi-source heterogeneous data. The result is that they fail to fully extract the common semantics and complementary information among multi-source heterogeneous data. To address this issue, we present MGACMR, an multigranularity adversarial cross-modal retrieval model. In this model, First, we embed the attention mechanism into the adversarial subspace learning network and construct a generative adversarial network for local multi-granularity feature fusion to jointly learn semantic-consistent representations within and across modalities. Second, we designed a cross-modal fine-grained semantic learning network based on the collaborative attention mechanism to enhance the complementary capability of semantic features within the subspace by associative guiding between the different semantics across modalities. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach outperforms similar cross-modal retrieval methods in terms of the Mean Average Precision metric.

title: Intrusion Event Recognition in Distributed Fiber-Optic Sensing Systems via Domain Adaptation Learning
authors: Yuxiang Ni, Linling Wang, Di Wu, Hailong Zhai, Xing Hu, Jing Cheng, Dawei Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: With the extensive deployment of distributed fiber vibration sensing (DVS) systems in security monitoring and structural health diagnostics, conventional recognition algorithms encounter three critical challenges in cross-domain scenarios: environmental noise discrepancy, domain shift, and prohibitive annotation costs. To overcome these limitations, this study presents a novel cross-domain fiber vibration recognition framework leveraging deep domain adaptation. Our architecture integrates a hybrid distribution alignment mechanism combining CORAL (CORrelation Alignment) and MMD (Maximum Mean Discrepancy) metrics, synergistically enhanced by adversarial training and dynamic optimization strategies to achieve robust spatiotemporal feature learning across indoor/outdoor environments. The proposed hierarchical residual-convolutional network extracts multi-scale time-frequency representations while simultaneously minimizing inter-domain divergence through joint optimization of three objective functions: categorical crossentropy, adversarial loss, and statistical moment matching constraints. Comprehensive ablation studies demonstrate the superiority of our framework over baseline Softmax classifiers and single-alignment variants, with quantitative evaluations revealing significant performance improvements in cross-domain generalization. This work establishes a generalized paradigm for optical vibration monitoring in complex field conditions, advancing the practical implementation of intelligent fiber sensing technologies in industrial applications.

title: Analysis of spectral responses in soybean crops with different levels of phytonematode infestation at different phenological stages using MSI/Sentinel-2 sensor imagery
authors: Glória Maria Padovani Ederli, Aluir Porfírio Dal Poz, Nilton Nobuhiro Imai, Adilson Berveglieri, Fábio Fernando Araujo, Gabriela Lozano Olivério Salvador, Eija Honkavaara
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: Phytonematodes are parasites of plant tissues, primarily roots. Although they cause significant damage to agriculture, they are still underexplored. Soybean crops suffer significant yield losses due to phytonematode infestations. Remote sensing techniques offer an effective means of detecting and analyzing these infestations. This study aimed to identify the most suitable stage in the soybean plant’s growth cycle for detecting spectral differences between nematode-infested and healthy plants using MSI/Sentinel-2 imagery. The study utilized three sets of Sentinel-2 images, representing different growth stages (December - vegetative, January - grain development, February - maturation). 38 nematological sample points were classified into three infestation levels (no or low, moderate, high). Spectral responses were collected for each growth stage, followed by an analysis of reflectance values and NDVI to assess class separability. The maturation stage exhibited the most significant spectral differences between infestation levels, especially in the near-infrared and red-edge regions. The sum of squared differences demonstrated the greatest separability in the maturation stage, particularly between moderate and no or low infestation categories. The results suggest that the end of the reproductive stage is optimal for detecting spectral differences in soybean plants affected by nematodes.

title: Design and Application of an Evaluation System for Large Language Models
authors: Ying Shen, Zhiyuan Cao, Mingang Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: With the widespread adoption of Large Language Models (LLMs) across various domains, establishing an evaluation system has become increasingly critical. However, existing mainstream evaluation methodologies still suffer from limitations in both evaluation datasets and evaluation methods. To address these limitations, we propose an innovative evaluation system for LLMs. The system comprises four core components: (1) definition of evaluation dimensions, (2) construction of evaluation datasets, (3) automated interaction, and (4) automated evaluation, together forming a complete closed-loop evaluation pipeline. Notably, to bridge the gap between existing evaluation benchmarks and the latest standards, we construct a multi-dimensional evaluation dataset fully aligned with those standards. Based on this dataset, we perform a comprehensive evaluation of multiple LLMs, systematically revealing their performance across dimensions defined by standards.

title: Enhanced Text Extraction from Low-Quality Legacy Documents Using TrOCR Assisted by Line Detection
authors: A Kirti Meghana, Harsh Gupta, Preeti Dubey
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: Legacy documents are mostly found in unstructured formats that make them difficult to retrieve digitally. For Microsoft’s TrOCR model, we used a domain-specific fine-tuning method paired with a line detection model using detectron2 framework and post OCR correction using SymSpell to improve the detection of low-resolution, faded, and degraded text. The data were collected from archived Indian government documents. Using mixed-precision training on constrained resources, the model was trained on our proprietary dataset, which was annotated line-wise. With a character error rate of 7.01 and a word error rate of 14.21 on the validation set, the model showed a very significant improvement over the base version. Our findings demonstrate the great efficacy of specifically pretrained OCR model fine-tuning for the large-scale restoration and digitization of important historical data.

title: Boost or Block? Exploring the Impact Mechanisms of Deep Learning in GenAI Interactions
authors: Jiaying Mo, Shaodong Peng, Chunyan Guo, Jie Gao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: In the era background where Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) is deeply integrated into students' learning, college students are increasingly relying on it in their learning processes. GenAI has “advantages” such as enhancing learning efficiency and promoting knowledge construction, but it may also cause “worries” such as mental inertia and the deterioration of critical thinking. To clarify the impact mechanisms of GenAI interaction on deep learning, this study takes the “Gen-AIinteractive classroom” as the research context, and adopts a mixed-method approach that combines quantitative surveys and classroom observations to explore the occurrence process of the influence mechanism and of GenAI interaction on college students' deep learning. The study constructs an influence mechanism model with “intelligent learning ability” and “critical thinking” as mediating variables. The results show that high-quality GenAI interaction can promote the generation of deep learning among college students. Among them, intelligent learning ability plays a partial mediating role, critical thinking plays a significant mediating role, and the moderating effect of cognitive load is not significant. This study reveals the “black box” path of GenAI interactions promoting deep learning, offering both theoretical insights and practical implications for building future-oriented intelligent learning environments and optimizing human-AI collaborative teaching.

title: Experimental Characterization of Soft Materials for Robotic Applications
authors: Sitan Wang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: This study investigates the mechanical performance and degradation of two soft-robotic elastomers-Agilus 30, produced via PolyJet 3D printing, and Dragon Skin 30, obtained through conventional mold casting. Standard ISO 527-2-2012 tensile and cyclic tests revealed that over 12 weeks, Agilus 30 lost approximately 15% of its tensile strength and 12% of its stiffness. In contrast, Dragon Skin 30 experienced a more moderate decline, with a 7% reduction in tensile strength and 5% in stiffness. This highlights the impact of the fabrication method and curing protocol on long-term durability. Moreover, Finite-element analyses in Abaqus, employing hyperelastic Ogden models, reproduced the initial stress-strain response accurately and showed that a 2D plane-stress surrogate can cut computational cost relative to full 3D solids. The combined experimental-numerical findings in this report suggest that optimized post-cure regimes and structural reinforcements are necessary to extend service life: Dragon Skin 30 is preferred for applications requiring long-term mechanical stability, while Agilus 30 remains advantageous for rapid, geometry-rich prototyping.

title: AI-Enabled Application Mode of Automated Assessment for Online Collaborative Learning Based on Knowledge Graphs
authors: Hongpeng Zhang, Shu Zhang, Mingjie Wang, Wei Zou
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Artificial intelligence is driving innovation in education, particularly in automated assessment for collaborative learning. This study proposes an AI-enabled application mode of automated assessment based on knowledge graphs. The mode integrates a syllabus-derived target knowledge graph with group graphs generated from discussions, and evaluates group learning through graph-theoretic indicators (node count, degree, path length) and semantic matching metrics (similarity, closeness, range consistency). A quasi-experimental design in an undergraduate computer course compared an experimental group with a control group. ANCOVA and sequential analysis revealed that the experimental group achieved significantly deeper knowledge construction, higher group performance, and more systematic shared regulation behaviors. The results validate the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed AI-enabled mode and highlight its potential to support intelligent, scalable, and timely assessment in online collaborative learning.

title: An Optimization Method for LLM-Based Retrieval-Augmented Generation System on OpenWebUI
authors: Yuejiao Wang, Haochen Zhang, Shen Li, Meijuan Zhou, Zhong Ma
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems integrate retrieval mechanisms with generative models to enhance the output of generative models by sourcing relevant information from external knowledge sources. However, redundancy and noise in massive documents can interfere with large models to identify key information, leading to generation errors. This study proposes an optimized RAG framework leveraging OpenWebUI, implementing three key innovations: First, to mitigate semantic deviations in query rewriting, the original query is used as a set of retriever input, both original and rewritten queries are concurrently submitted to the retrieval system. Second, considering the risk that duplicate documents with smaller semantic distance will be filtered out in advance, a rearrangement and deduplication strategy based on semantic distance is designed to give priority to retaining document chunks with smaller distances. Finally, the BGE-M3 model-featuring extended text capacity and higher dimensionality - is deployed as a semantic vector model on the OpenWebUI platform, enabling sophisticated semantic matching through enhanced embedding vector representations. Experimental results demonstrate significant enhancement in retrieval precision and generation quality. The proposed approach exhibits substantial application potential for RAG system architecture and large model deployment, offering strong support for the performance improvement of RAG systems in practical implementations.

title: Research on Scientific Paper Writing for Graduate Students Based on Group Case-Based Practice
authors: Dan Zhang, Quanhong Peng, Dong Zhao, Jiawei Wang, Xiujuan Ma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Scientific Paper Writing is a course designed for master's and doctoral students, aiming to improve their practical skills in academic communication and scientific writing. The course addresses common problems in research writing, such as unclear structure, improper language use, weak logic, lack of submission strategy, and insufficient awareness of academic ethics. It adopts a teaching model that combines theoretical instruction with practical training. A key component of the practical module is the “group-based paper comparison and rewriting task,” in which students select three journal articles of different quality levels for comparative analysis and paragraph rewriting. This approach enhances students' critical reading and writing transfer abilities. This paper focuses on the implementation of group case analysis within the course and examines its effectiveness in improving graduate students' scientific writing skills, offering insights for future instructional design.

title: Research on tamper-proof and privacy protection scheme of industrial MQTT data based on AES key dynamic derivation
authors: Huixin Jin, Xiangri Qu, Lu Lin, Xiaotong Yuan, Yuan Jin
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: To address the security and performance limitation s of MQTT in Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) applications, th is paper proposes an optimized AES encryption scheme. It utilizes Curve25519 Diffie-Hellman for secure key exchange and mutual authentication. An adaptive mechanism employs AES-GCM to en sure confidentiality and integrity for critical control data, while o ptimized AES-CBC with HMAC-SHA256-derived initialization v ectors (IVs) is used for efficient transmission of monitoring data. Additionally, dynamic session key updates enhance security by pr otecting against replay attacks and key leakage, thereby meeting i ndustrial requirements for low latency and high concurrency.

title: The Impact of RCEP on Member Countries' Digital Services Exports: An Empirical Analysis Based on a Two-Way Fixed Effects Model
authors: Qiuping Wu
publish: Proceedings of the International Conference on Digital Economy and Information Technology
abstract: None

title: A Multimodal Privacy Filtering System using Deep Learning for Visual-Audio Input Streams
authors: KunWoo Kim, Sang-won Park, Hyeon-Ju Lee, Hoyong Choi, Seok-Jun Buu
abstract: Protecting personally identifiable information (PII) in visual and audio data streams that are continuously being captured by sensing systems remains a critical challenge. Devices such as IoT cameras and smart microphones routinely capture sensitive elements such as faces, voices, and behavioral or contextual cues, often without the subject's awareness or consent. To address this issue, we propose a multimodal PII filtering system designed for privacy protection in environments where visual and auditory data are persistently recorded. The proposed system detects and irreversibly anonymizes PII at the point of capture, before the data is transmitted or stored in vulnerable infrastructure. It incorporates a visual object detection module based on YOLOv12 and a sentence-level classifier based on BERT, applied to speech transcriptions generated by a speech-to-text module. These modules operate within a unit-based processing framework that segments incoming data into short temporal units, enabling low-latency operation while maintaining semantic consistency across modalities. Although each modality is processed independently, the system maintains temporal synchronization to ensure coherent filtering decisions. We evaluate the system using both in-house and public datasets across diverse conditions including variable lighting and background noise. The system achieves a unified false negative rate of about 3%, showing reliable performance for real-world multimodal privacy protection. Furthermore, the system employs parallel unit-based processing to maximize computational efficiency, and its modular design supports flexible component combinations, confirming suitability for edge or cloud deployment. These findings demonstrate that the proposed system provides an efficient and scalable solution for real-world multimodal privacy protection.

title: Dynamics-Based Adaptive Scheduling for Large-Scale Edge Computing Networks in Smart Cities
authors: Yixiao Feng, Yang Chen, Zhen-Zhong Hu, Zhengru Ren
abstract: The rapid proliferation of Internet of Things (IoT) devices in smart cities has led to an exponential growth in task requests, posing significant challenges to real-time and efficient task scheduling within large-scale distributed edge computing networks. This paper proposes a novel dynamics-based adaptive scheduling (DAS) method to address this NP-hard problem. The core innovation of DAS lies in its transformation of the complex task scheduling challenge into a two-dimensional geometric model. By leveraging the principles of forward dynamics simulation, DAS achieves efficient and non-iterative task assignment. This approach avoids the reliance on iterative optimization or complex model training, successfully reducing the computational complexity to O(n log n), which makes it highly suitable for large-scale, dynamic smart city edge networks. Simulation results demonstrate that the DAS method significantly outperforms existing benchmark strategies across key performance indicators. Specifically, DAS reduces service response time by 9% to 15%, improves system load balanceooo by 93% to 98%, and simultaneously decreases overall energy consumption by 2% to 12%. These quantitative findings strongly validate that DAS provides an efficient, scalable, and real-time responsive scheduling solution for large-scale edge computing environments, substantially enhancing the performance and reliability of smart city services.

title: A Lithium Niobate Piezoelectric Micromachined Ultrasonic Transducer Operating in Thickness-Field-Excitation ModeWith Enhanced Acoustic Emission
authors: Zhiquan Liu, Xianhao Le, Xiongfeng Wang, Yang Jiang, Qing Wan, Wei Xu, Junfa Mao, Wugang Liao, Feng Gao
abstract: Lead-free piezoelectric materials have garnered increasing attention as environmentally friendly alternatives to lead-based transducers. Among them, lithium niobate (LN) is a promising candidate. However, the transmit displacement sensitivity of existing LN piezoelectric micromachined ultrasonic transducers (PMUTs) remains significantly lower than that of PZT devices due to the limitation of lateral-field-excitation (LFE). In this work, an LN PMUT operating in the thickness-field- excitation (TFE) mode was designed and fabricated on a 41° Y-cut lithium niobate on insulator (LNOI) substrate. The bottom electrode was innovatively fabricated by deep reactive ion etching and magnetron sputtering, which simplifies the fabrication process and reduces cost compared with conventional methods. The device achieves a transmit sensitivity of 460.3nm/V at an operating frequency of 400kHz, surpassing previously reported LFE LN PMUTs. [2025-0247]

title: Adaptive Clustering Convexification Mapping-Planning System for UAVs in Unknown Environments
authors: Yifei Zhang, Zheng Tan, Kang Liu, Yefeng Yang, Wenyu Yang, Shiyuan Wang, Chih-Yung Wen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: In complex environments, available moving space is often non-convex due to the presence of irregular obstacles, which complicates the mapping module’s ability to represent the environment with geometric symbols. This complexity hampers the effectiveness of the planning module, leading to increased computational and storage demands. To address these challenges posed by non-convex spaces, we propose an adaptive clustering convexification mapping-planning system (ACCMPS) for unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) navigating in unknown environments. Our approach introduces an adaptive space-searching algorithm that transforms non-convex areas into relatively convex sub-spaces represented by clusters through purposeful sampling. ACCMPS utilizes a sample-friendly 3D grid map for local mapping and constructs a storage-efficient cluster-based weighted graph for global representation, linking convex sub-spaces represented by clusters. This integration streamlines the mapping and path planning processes, enabling direct initial path generation from the weighted graph, rather than relying on traditional algorithms that blindly explore non-convex spaces. To optimize paths, we employ a particle filter on the initial graph-derived path to handle non-convexity, followed by optimization into a smooth Bspline trajectory via cost function minimization within relatively convex regions. A series of real-world experiments conducted in both narrow and expansive environments demonstrate the advantages of ACCMPS in terms of computational efficiency, storage consumption, and path quality compared to existing state-of-the-art methods.

title: Real-Time Deep Reinforcement Learning Policy Controller for Magnetically Levitated Robots
authors: Yang Wang, Yanze Li, Sebastian Viancha, Mir Behrad Khamesee
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Magnetic levitation (Maglev) technology is widely adopted for transportation, photolithography, clean room, and material-handling applications, owing to its vibration- and friction-free, untethered motion. The magnetic movers are actuated using the stator coil array, which functions as mobile maglev robots (MLRs) with precision position and orientation motion control. Therefore, this article studies the MLRs for intelligent manufacturing, such as material handling and collaboration with manufacturing components, by integrating them with deep reinforcement learning (DRL) policy controllers. Linear DRL policies are developed and compared between Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) and Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) for the horizontal motion control, which takes 455 μs to compute the actuating forces for real-time operations. During policy training, random magnitude horizontal step responses are ticked every 500 ms to explore and exploit the dynamic capacity of the MLRs. The PPO policies are finalized based on the discovered deterministic nature of the maglev environment. Furthermore, the symmetry of the MLR configuration is utilized for policy training, with a threshold boundary included to enable MLRs to translate smoothly across the horizontal operating range. The trained policies exhibit aggressive oscillations and persistent offsets relative to references, respectively. To alleviate the fitting problem, distance-based soft state machines are introduced to integrate the DRL policy with the basic controller. The state machine parameters are empirically tuned using various location step responses. This feature enables the dynamic responses of the DRL policy to be combined with the precision responses at references. The soft state machines are also trained as auxiliary policy actions and evaluated using full operating-range trajectory tracking and long-stroke step responses alongside the policy and the basic controller. The DRL policy controllers outperform the basic controllers, with improved rise and settling times in step responses, while showing enhanced performance in tracking trajectories. Finally, the policy robustness is empirically evaluated.

title: A Unified Framework for Group Delay Flattening: Stable and Hardware-Efficient All-Pass Networks
authors: Raghav Krishna, Ishan Aryendu, Sudhanshu Arya, Ying Wang
abstract: This paper presents a unified framework for group delay (GD) equalization and phase distortion compensation using cascaded all-pass filter (APF) networks. The proposed approach addresses the critical limitation of finite impulse response (FIR) equalizers, which require prohibitively high orders for adequate wideband and multiband performance. We consider GD flattening as a stability-constrained optimization problem, adjusting APF poles to minimize root mean square and maximum GD deviations over prescribed frequency bands. A hybrid optimization strategy ensures robust convergence to high-quality solutions. The framework is extensively benchmarked against FIR and spline-based equalizers, demonstrating superior GD flatness and lower phase distortion at a fraction of the computational complexity. Results across single-band, multiband, and wideband scenarios show that the APF equalizers reduce GD error by orders of magnitude and remain robust under noise and coefficient quantization. Furthermore, we introduce a differentiable extension of the APF cascade, trainable with the Adam optimizer. This differentiable Adam-based extension demonstrates faster adaptations. At relaxed error levels, both classical re-optimization and Adam-based fine-tuning converge within a small number of iterations. Hardware feasibility is confirmed through FPGA synthesis and CMOS-level analysis, highlighting low latency and resource efficiency. The proposed solution establishes a practical, high-performance path for deployable phase equalization in modern communication, radar, and biomedical systems.

title: Learning Category-Invariant Disentangled Features for Domain Generalization in Machine Fault Diagnosis
authors: Zuoyi Chen, Jun Wu, Zhongwei Deng, Hong-Zhong Huang
abstract: Existing domain generalization (DG) methods typically align multidomain data into a shared feature space to extract domain-invariant representations for machine fault diagnosis. Departing from this alignment paradigm, this article proposes a novel learning category-invariant features (LCIFs) framework. Motivated by the concept of genetic markers, the LCIF explicitly decomposes machine health states into domain attributes (reflecting operating conditions) and category attributes (reflecting fault types), and disentangles category-invariant features from multidomain observations. To this end, a patch-level feature adaptive aggregation is built in the feature extraction module to suppress noise attributes, and learnable condition masks are constructed to capture multigranularity local and global features. Parallel attribute disentanglement branches are then established to extract category and domain features separately. For the category attribute branch, an Earth Mover’s Distance based cross-attention mechanism is designed to strengthen category-specific channels and suppress domain interference. Experiments on public benchmarks and a self-collected dataset, covering single and multiple unseen domain diagnostic tasks, show that the LCIF substantially outperforms several state-of-the-art DG methods on unseen operating conditions.
