title: Method for Extracting Dynamic Human Body Contour Features from Yoga Teaching and Training Videos
authors: Chen Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: To address the issues of inaccurate contour extraction and low joint recognition accuracy in traditional human body contour feature extraction methods, this paper proposes a research on dynamic human body contour feature extraction method. Firstly, preprocess the video data of yoga teaching and training; Secondly, motion regions are detected in the predicted video, and a sliding window structure is designed to segment the yoga action sequence, dividing the video into several action units; Then, using deep transfer learning methods, a human yoga action recognition model is established, and new behavior samples from the target domain are input to match yoga action features; Finally, establish an OpenPose skeleton model to identify dynamic human joint points and extract dynamic human contour features; Convert contour features into parameter form and map them to human joint points. The experimental results show that as the number of video frames gradually increases, the center position error of the target extracted from human dynamic features remains between 2.0 and 2.3, with small fluctuations and high stability. This indicates that the application of this method not only better captures the overall shape of the human body, but also demonstrates higher accuracy in identifying human joint points.

title: A Deep Learning-Based Inverse Design Framework for Microscale FGM Pressure Sensor Diaphragms
authors: Bing Han, Xiongzhu Bu, Fan Chen, Maojun Fan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: This work establishes a dedicated and complicated deep-learning-based inverse model for designing FGM microscale pressure-sensing diaphragm. A high-speed surrogate model has been created using a large, high-fidelity dataset obtained via training a deep neural network. In addition, crucial data were taken from an improved refined plate theory which accounts for microlevel plastification through nonlocal strain gradient effect, physics can accurately present this. In comparison with ordinary forward modeling, our way of processing problems could perfectly present us the most qualified diaphragm form if we determined certain specific sensitivity values and linearity standards; thereby avoiding much bothersome iterative numerical simulations involved in the whole design process. Besides, our framework possesses highly superior predicting accuracy whilst resolving sensibility-linearity conflict efficiently as well.

title: How Generative Artificial Intelligence Empowers Teaching: A Systematic Literature Review of Empirical Evidence from China
authors: Jihong Li, Huaibo Wang, Peng Chen, Qingqing Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: With the deep penetration of Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) technologies into the educational domain, it has transitioned from initial conceptual exploration to large-scale practical implementation, becoming a key driver in reshaping pedagogical paradigms and addressing traditional instructional challenges. To systematically clarify the application patterns of GenAI in teaching contexts, this study conducts a systematic literature review of 48 empirical papers from CNKI's CSSCI and Peking University Core databases. The research focuses on identifying the key problems GenAI solves in traditional education, the instructional stages and roles it engages in, the teaching objectives it supports, and the differences in its application across educational levels and disciplines. Results indicate that GenAI prioritizes addressing core pain points such as delayed/insufficient feedback and scarce/slow-updating resources. It has permeated the entire teaching process and dynamically assumes multiple roles. Moreover, GenAI demonstrates significant effectiveness in supporting higher-order objectives in Bloom's taxonomy. Its application models are highly aligned with the educational positioning of different levels and the epistemological attributes of different disciplines, exhibiting differentiated characteristics. Finally, practical implications are proposed from three perspectives: scenario matching, teacher positioning, and risk management, providing guidance for the precise application of GenAI in teaching.

title: Dynamic Beam Hopping Management for the LEO Satellite Communications Using the LNN
authors: Yue Wang, Xiumei Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: The low earth orbit (LEO) satellite plays a pivotal role in the development of the sixth-generation (6 G) communication system. However, the high-speed mobility of the LEO satellite poses significant challenges to reliable communication between the satellite and its ground users. Furthermore, the limited onboard resource of the satellite necessitates an efficient dynamic resource management strategy. To address these challenges, we design a dynamic beam hopping scheme based on the liquid neural network (LNN) to enhance system throughput and user delay performance. Beam illumination patterns and beam-user allocations are determined by the LNN-based scheme. Simulation results demonstrate that the LNN-based scheme effectively improves both system throughput and user delay satisfaction compared to several baseline schemes.

title: Research on the Pathways and Impacts of Reforming Legal Practice Education Empowered by Artificial Intelligence
authors: Meng Xu, Jianxun Guo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has emerged as a driving force for innovation in legal practice education in China. Focusing on key practical arenas such as moot courts, legal clinics, and case-based training, this paper systematically reviews the structural transformation, capacity enhancement, and risk challenges brought by AI empowerment. Utilizing authentic cases from higher education institutions and empirical survey data, this article systematically examines the five interconnected dimensions of reform: pathways, mechanisms, outcomes, risks, and strategic responses. Findings indicate that Artificial Intelligence markedly enhances law students' abilities in analysis, innovation, and collaboration, facilitating the shift of legal education from knowledge transmission to competency-based and intelligent collaborative models. However, the integration of AI also introduces new challenges, such as technological dependency, ethical governance, and regional disparities. This article proposes pathways for regional cooperation, faculty development, ethical governance, and competency-oriented reforms, aiming to provide both theoretical support and practical models for the intelligent, collaborative, and high-quality development of legal practice education in China.

title: Research on Deep Reinforcement Learning Optimization Methods in State Perception and Fault Location of Power Distribution Network Equipment
authors: Ye Zhou, Siyuan Dai, Na Liu, Chao Zhou, Qichong Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: The development of intelligent power distribution networks has put forward higher requirements for real-time performance and accuracy in equipment status perception and fault location. This paper combines deep reinforcement learning technology to construct a multi-source perception data fusion model and uses time series modeling and knowledge graphs to optimize recognition and location. In response to issues such as perception blind spots, signal interference, insufficient samples, and unstable policy learning, this paper proposes an optimized path of lightweight deployment and transfer learning, providing strong support for the safe operation and autonomous response capability improvement of distribution networks in complex environments.

title: The Technology Capital Accumulation and Dissemination Model Based on Complex Networks in the Digital Background
authors: Fan Zhang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: The continuous development of the digital age makes technological capital the most important driving force for social progress. Based on the theory of complex networks, this paper establishes a model for the accumulation and dissemination of technological capital, and explains the influence of different network structures on the accumulation rate and dissemination efficiency of technological capital. By setting node attributes, propagation mechanisms, and the rate function of technological capital accumulation, combined with network graphs and delay effects, the changing laws of technological capital in the digital background are understood from a new perspective. Mathematical simulation and parameter sensitivity tests are utilized to verify the influence of different network structures on the transmission effect of technological capital. It provides a scientific basis for the social development strategies and enterprise technological innovation strategies in the digital age.

title: Sequential Cooperative Multi-Agent Online Learning and Adaptive Coordination Control in Dynamic and Uncertain Environments
authors: Limengxi Yue, Duo Xu, Dong Qiu, Yanpei Shi, Shuyang Xu, Manish Shah
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Dynamic multi-agent systems must coordinate under partial information, time-varying disturbances, and abrupt non-stationarity while satisfying hard safety constraints. This paper proposes a sequential cooperative multi-agent online learning and adaptive coordination control framework for ordered missions. A task graph encodes precedence relations and activates stage-specific objectives, linking a global goal to a sequence of subtasks. On this structure, each agent runs a distributed online actor-critic update using local observations and event-triggered neighbor messages. The learned nominal inputs are then wrapped by a minimally invasive quadratic-program (QP) safety filter that enforces collision avoidance, formation/tracking constraints, and input saturation in real time, while an adaptive/robust term compensates bounded disturbances. Lyapunov-based analysis establishes uniform ultimate boundedness of the closed-loop signals and convergence of the online policies to a neighborhood of a cooperative optimum under mild conditions. In simulations on multi-robot formation tracking, dynamic target encirclement, and cooperative payload transportation (200 runs), the proposed method achieves 94.7% ± 2.6% task success, outperforming centralized MPC/DMPC (88.9% ± 3.7%) and single-stage safe MARL (86.3% ± 4.3%). It reduces average convergence time to 23.4 ± 4.1 s (vs. 28.8 ± 4.9 s for centralized MPC/DMPC) while maintaining zero safety violations. Event-triggered communication lowers the message rate to 3.2 msgs/(agent·s), compared with 10.0 msgs/(agent·s) under periodic-communication baselines, without degrading completion performance.

title: Optimized Image-Based Disease Detection: Evaluating the Effects of Preprocessing, Annotation Methods, and Dataset Variability
authors: Harold Y. Bangalisan, J. Adeline Sneha, V. Sivakumar
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: Calamansi (Citrofortunella microcarpa), a vital citrus crop in the Philippines, is increasingly threatened by leaf diseases such as Huanglongbing (HLB), which severely impact yield and quality. While artificial intelligence (AI) and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) have shown potential for automated plant disease detection, most models are trained on ideal, studio-quality images with labor-intensive manual annotation, limiting their applicability in real-world farming conditions. This study investigates how three key factors: image enhancement using Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE), AI-assisted annotation, and dataset condition (controlled vs. uncontrolled) affect the performance of CNNs in calamansi leaf disease detection. A $2 \times 2 \times 2$ factorial experiment was conducted using 3,000 annotated images collected from farms in Oriental Mindoro, Philippines. These images were utilized in both training and evaluation phases of the four deep learning models: YOLOv11, YOLOv12, Faster R-CNN, and RF-DETR. Results show that CLAHE significantly improved model accuracy under uneven lighting by 4.8%, while AI-assisted labeling reduced annotation time by over 70% with minimal loss in precision. Among the models, YOLOv12 achieved the best overall balance of accuracy and speed (mAP@50:95 = 64.2%; Recall = 73.6% with a variation of 0.9), making it most suitable for scenarios prioritizing sensitivity. RF-DETR achieved the highest precision (72.3% with a variation of 0.5), underscoring its strength in minimizing false positives and providing reliable detections. This study underscores the value of preprocessing, semi-automated labeling, and realistic field datasets in building robust, deployable AI systems for plant disease detection, contributing to practical, low-cost solutions for resource-constrained agricultural environments.

title: Personalized Recommendation of Human Resource Education and Training Content Based on Deep Improvement Neural Networks
authors: Lan Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Traditional personalized recommendations for human resources education and training content rely heavily on content similarity, resulting in poor matching performance and difficulty in meeting the diverse needs of users. Therefore, this study proposes a personalized recommendation method based on deep neural network optimization. First, the features related to employee competence are selected, and the vector space model is used to construct an employee competence portrait; human resource education and training content information is mined, and the multimodal features of the content are extracted and fused to comprehensively characterize the content properties; a deep improved neural network model is constructed, and a personalized recommendation list is generated by learning the mapping relationship between the user features and the training content through training. The experimental results show that after the application of this method, the fit between recommended content and user preferences exceeds 90%, and the Gini coefficient remains stable at a low level of 0.17, accurately meeting user needs, and the recommendation results are fair and reasonable.

title: An Accurate Recommendation Method for Online Educational Resources in Sports Based on User Profiles
authors: Qingwei Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: In the field of sports online educational resources recommendation, the traditional method is mainly realized based on the principle of content filtering, which has the problems of insufficient recommendation accuracy and lack of resource diversity. Therefore, a study on the accurate recommendation method of sports online educational resources based on user profiles is proposed. First, user static information labels and dynamic information labels are selected to construct a user data labeling system and extract key features. A user portrait model is constructed to comprehensively portray user interests and preferences and accurately reflect the real needs of users. Using the constructed user image, combined with the characteristics of sports online educational resources, the accurate recommendation of resources is realized by matching the user image and resource characteristics. The experimental results show that the method achieves more than 95 % matching of recommended resources among all 10 users, which significantly improves the accuracy of recommendation and can more accurately capture user needs. Meanwhile, with the increase of the number of educational resources, the Shannon Diversity Index shows a rising trend, indicating that the method can effectively provide diversified recommended resources while maintaining high accuracy.

title: Construction of a Korean Semantic Dependency Analysis Algorithm for Large-Scale Corpora
authors: Min Yuan
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: Korean semantic dependency analysis is an important part in natural language processing, especially for large-scale corpora, where there are many problems and challenges. This paper analyzes the Korean semantic dependency analysis algorithm for large-scale corpora, designs and implements an efficient algorithm model. The challenges existing in the semantic dependency analysis of the Korean language are proposed, and a data processing flow is designed for large-scale corpora. Corresponding solutions were provided for the above problems. The algorithm design was analyzed from multiple aspects, such as multi-task learning, transfer learning, data augmentation and other technical methods. Tests were conducted on some standard datasets. The experimental results show that this algorithm model can effectively improve the accuracy and efficiency of semantic dependency analysis of the Korean language.

title: Deep Learning on UAV Imagery: Applications Across Civil and Military Domains
authors: Rhaclley Franklein Ferreira Araújo, André Laurindo Maitelli, Everton José Castro Rego, Rodrigo Kuntz Rangel, Gilberto Cugler, Eduardo Fonseca
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: Over the past years, unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) have become essential tools for inspection and monitoring tasks in various sectors due to their versatility, low operational costs, and ability to carry a wide range of sensors with which they can be equipped. This paper presents an integrated dualuse methodology that combines UAV-based imagery and deep learning to address both civil and military needs. On the civilian side, the approach supports energy utilities by combining photogrammetry and the YOLOv5 object detection model to automatically identify electrical grid components, while semantic segmentation of orthophotomosaics enables the detection of urban expansion and potentially unauthorized energy use near power infrastructures. On the military side, the same pipeline enhances situational awareness by enabling object detection and tracking in aerial reconnaissance imagery and converting these detections into standardized military symbology for improved decision support and mission planning. This dual application demonstrates how combining UAV imaging, computer vision, and geospatial intelligence can simultaneously strengthen infrastructure management and security operations in complex environments.

title: A Disciplining Method for VC-TCXO in Embedded Systems
authors: Yanpo Han, Wei Xin, Xiulei Lu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: In distributed seismic acquisition systems, the frequency stability of the ADC oscillator directly determines the time synchronization accuracy among multiple devices. To meet the requirements of multi-device time synchronization, this paper presents a voltage-controlled temperature-compensated crystal oscillator (VC-TCXO) disciplining method based on an embedded system. Using the pulse-per-second (PPS) signal from GPS as a time reference, a precise time window is constructed. A microcontroller unit (MCU) performs multi-window counting of the VC-TCXO output to obtain the frequency error, and a proportional-integral-derivative (PID) algorithm is employed to adjust the control voltage and discipline the oscillator. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method can rapidly constrain the oscillator frequency drift to within 0.1 ppm at room temperature and significantly suppress the accumulation of frequency errors caused by temperature variations and longterm operation, thereby improving frequency stability and enhancing the synchronization accuracy of seismic acquisition systems.

title: Multi-Object Tracking Algorithm for Infrared Images Based on Multi-Scale Large-Kernel Attention
authors: Jiabo Zhang, Yue Dai, Qian Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: This paper addresses the challenges of texture deficiency and thermal interference in infrared target tracking by proposing an enhanced multi-object tracking approach. Building upon the ByteTrack framework, the method introduces three key innovations: a lightweight multi-scale large-kernel attention mechanism for improved feature representation through hierarchical extraction; a gated spatial attention unit to refine feature selection; a dual-path feature fusion network that enhances thermal characteristic modeling. The YOLOv8 detector incorporating adaptive feature association shows notable improvements in infrared target tracking, especially under occlusion and low-contrast conditions. Benchmark evaluations confirm its enhanced detection precision and target identity consistency over traditional tracking approaches.

title: AFCNN-BiLSTM: A Model of Teaching Emotional Interaction Recognition Based on Multi-Modal Interactive Features
authors: Jie Chu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: The effective identification and analysis of teaching emotional interaction can facilitate timely adjustments in pedagogical approaches, thereby enhancing the overall quality of education. Due to the low recognition accuracy of teaching emotional interaction in complex teaching environment, this study proposes an intelligent recognition method that integrates multimodal interactive features to enhance the accuracy of teaching emotional interaction recognition. Firstly, the qualities of audio signals and facial images are improved through preprocessing. Then, the rhythmic features and spectral features of audio signals, as well as global depth features and Local Binary Patterns (LBP) features of facial expression images, are extracted to present teaching emotion. Finally, a model of teaching emotional interaction recognition based on multi-modal interactive features named “AFCNN-BiLSTM” is designed, which fuses both audio and facial emotion features to achieve teaching emotional interaction recognition. Experimental results indicate that the multi-dimensional features derived from audio signals and facial images more effectively characterize teaching emotions. The proposed method achieves a recognition accuracy of 91.32% on the public RAVDESS dataset and 86.74% on the self-built TSAFEMO dataset, significantly surpassing the performance of other advanced models, thereby enhancing the accuracy of teaching emotional interaction recognition.

title: Research and Design of Fault Protection for EVPN VPWS and EVPN VPLS Bridge
authors: Changxue Li, Hongbin Ma
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: EVPN VPWS bridging EVPN VPLS enables seamless integration and interconnection between point-to-point and multi-point to multi-point services. In scenarios where the OLT at the network access side connects to the MAN via the primary interface, and the range of VLAN connected on the MAN side is unknown beforehand, the PW-VE bridging solution is not applicable. This is because the VLAN ID for the PW-VE subinterface cannot be configured.This paper proposes a bridging solution where EVPL instances are directly bound to BD. MER bridging sites do not require configuration of AC ports for EVPN. Access-side OLT connect to MAR via primary/secondary interfaces, achieving protection and recovery convergence times under 500 milliseconds during failures. It analyzes relevant functional requirements and details the design for specific implementation. Network deployment and environment testing validate the effectiveness of this solution.

title: An Assisted Medical Diagnosis Question Answering System Based on Knowledge Graphs
authors: Liu Fang, Chen Yang, Chen Jiansong
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of artificial intelligence technology, knowledge graphs are seeing increasingly widespread application in the medical field, offering new technological means for assisted medical diagnosis. However, current research still faces challenges such as difficulties in natural language understanding and insufficient knowledge reasoning capabilities. This paper designs and implements a knowledge graph-based question answering system for assisted medical diagnosis, aiming to enhance diagnostic efficiency and accuracy through structured medical knowledge. The system first uses natural language processing techniques to analyze user-input medical questions, and then performs semantic reasoning by leveraging entities such as diseases, symptoms, medications, and medical tests, along with their relationships in a medical knowledge graph, to generate reliable diagnostic suggestions. Through knowledge graph embedding and relationship path mining, the system achieves multi-hop reasoning and ensures the scientific validity of responses based on authoritative medical databases. Experimental results demonstrate that this system achieves high accuracy and practicality in diagnostic question answering tasks for common diseases. It can provide efficient decision support assistance for both doctors and patients while reducing the risk of misdiagnosis.

title: Electromagnetic Transient Simulations for Large-Scale Power Grids: A Utility Perspective
authors: Hossein Hooshyar, Reza Pourramezan, Thai Thanh Nguyen, Rahul Kadavil, Victor Daldegan Paduani, Jitendra Thapa, Tung Lam Nguyen, Xuheng Lin, Sagnik Basumallik
abstract: As the global power grid transforms, utilities face growing complexity in modeling, stability assessment, and operational planning. The increasing penetration of inverter-based resources (IBRs), power-electronics-interfaced devices (PEIDs), and distributed energy technologies exceeds the limits of traditional phasor-domain tools, which struggle to capture fast, nonlinear, and unbalanced behaviors. Electromagnetic transient (EMT) simulations have become essential, offering sub-cycle accuracy, detailed switching behavior, and multi-domain integration. EMT is now integrated into planning workflows, interconnection studies, protection coordination schemes, real-time platforms, and digital twin architectures, helping utilities, system operators, manufacturers, and research institutions address modern grid challenges at both device and system levels. This article provides a utility-focused overview of EMT simulation strategies for large-scale power grids, with a particular emphasis on practical implementation, model development and validation, hybrid co-simulation architectures, and digital twin integration. Drawing from real-world experience and evolving industry standards, the article also highlights use cases across the power system lifecycle from planning and protection to procurement and interconnection. Furthermore, it offers a roadmap for scaling EMT practices in support of the future grid reliability and performance.

title: Research on Lightning Protection Filters for Aircraft Power Supply Equipmentaper
authors: Dianguo Hou, Jiaming Pan, Chao Hu, Yaogen Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: The instantaneous interference energy generated by the indirect effect of lightning may cause equipment circuits to be shorted, components to be overloaded or even burned. In this paper, lightning indirect effect analysis and protection research on power supply system of aircraft are carried out. Compared with the traditional indirect lightning protection that uses multiple transient suppressors in series for voltage clamping, this paper simplifies the circuit design and uses a TVS series filter circuit method for indirect lightning protection. In this paper, the appropriate TVS device is selected to meet the requirements of protection levels 3, 4 and 5 specified in DO- 160G. In this paper, different filter circuits are designed for DC 270V DC and 115V AC Three-phase four-wire system. The TVS mainly clamps transient voltage. The filter circuit effectively filters out circuit noise and ensures that everything remains within the normal working range. The test results demonstrate a high degree of consistency between the insertion loss tests and the simulations. The indirect lightning protection performance meets the requirements of DO-160G, and the filter insulation resistance and voltage drop comply with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{GB} / \mathrm{T} 20240.5-2012$</tex>. The research findings offer valuable reference for the design of strong electromagnetic pulse protection for aircraft power supply equipment. Abstract text.

title: Reconfiguring Human Subjectivity in the Age of Generative Artificial Intelligence: A Philosophical Reflection and Evidence-Informed Framework
authors: Chang Chang, Duowen Chen, He Nan
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Conference on Human-Computer Interaction, Neural Networks and Deep Learning
abstract: None
